spec:
4-port round-robin arbiter with fair scheduling.

Inputs:
- clk: system clock (rising edge)
- rst: async active-high reset
- req[3:0]: request lines from 4 requestors (active high, multiple can be set)

Outputs:
- grant[3:0]: one-hot grant signal (at most 1 bit set at a time)
- valid: high when a grant is being issued

Behavior:
- Reset: grant=0000, valid=0, priority starts at requestor 0.
- Each clock cycle, grant the highest-priority pending request using round-robin.
- Round-robin: after granting requestor N, the next priority starts at requestor N+1 (wrapping 3→0).
- If no requests, grant=0000, valid=0. Priority pointer does not change.
- Grant is registered (appears 1 cycle after request changes).
- Grant is always one-hot (never grants two requestors simultaneously).

Test cases:
- Reset state: grant=0, valid=0
- Single request on req[0]: grant=0001, valid=1. Priority rotates to 1.
- Single request on req[2]: depends on current priority pointer.
- Simultaneous req[0] and req[2] with priority at 0: grants req[0] first, then req[2] next cycle.
- All 4 requesting: should cycle 0→1→2→3→0 over consecutive cycles.
- No requests: grant=0, valid=0, priority holds.
- Fairness: after req[3] granted, req[0] gets priority even if req[3] requests again.
- Back-to-back: continuous requests should show round-robin rotation.
